sections.title: What is a Continent?
points: A continent is a very large mass of land on the earth's surface.
There are seven continents in the world.
They are separated by large water bodies called Oceans.
Pangea: Before they separated by continental drifting, all continents were once one large supercontinent called Pangea.

sections.title: 1. Asia (The Giant)
points: Population: It has the highest population in the world.
Key Fact: It is home to the highest mountain peak, Mt. Everest.

sections.title: 2. Africa (Our Continent)
points: Position: It is the most central continent on the globe.
Fact: It has the most number of countries (55).

sections.title: 3. The Americas
points: North America: 3rd Largest. Contains USA and Canada.
South America: 4th Largest. Contains the Amazon Rainforest.
They are separated by the Panama Canal.

sections.title: 4. The Unique Continents
points: Antarctica (5th): The only continent uninhabited by permanent residents because it is too cold (frozen desert).
Europe (6th): Attached to Asia. Together they are called Eurasia.
Australia (7th): The Smallest continent. It is also an island country.

sections.text: Greenland is a very large island, but it is NOT a continent! It is geographically part of North America.

cards.fact: The combined landmass of Europe and Asia.
The process by which the earth's continents move relative to each other over geological time.
cards.mnemonic: Think of 'Drifting' boats moving apart in the ocean.
